title: Amauropelta frigida
taxonomy: Amauropelta frigida is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of fern native to South America. The classification of Amauropelta frigida includes its genus, Amauropelta, and species, frigida. This plant is part of the larger family of Polypodiaceae, which consists of various types of ferns. The classification of Amauropelta frigida is further divided into its kingdom, Phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species.

intro: Amauropelta frigida, also known as the frost fern, is a species of fern native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Amauropelta frigida include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
